summary: Azure Backup is a cloud-based backup service that protects on-premises and Azure resources by storing backups in Recovery Services vaults or Backup vaults. It supports a wide range of workloads including Azure VMs, SQL Server, SAP HANA, Azure Files, Blobs, PostgreSQL, and Kubernetes. It provides application-consistent backups, multiple storage redundancy options, and built-in ransomware protection.

key_concepts.explanation: A storage entity in Azure that holds backup data and recovery points for VMs, files, SQL databases, and other workloads. Supports short and long-term retention.
A newer vault type used for backing up specific workloads like Azure Managed Disks, PostgreSQL, and Kubernetes (AKS).
Microsoft Azure Recovery Services agent installed on-premises or on Azure VMs to back up files, folders, and system state directly to Azure.
Azure Backup Server (MABS) and Data Protection Manager (DPM) are agents used to protect on-premises VMs (Hyper-V, VMware) and workloads before sending to Azure.
A backup that captures all in-memory data and pending transactions so the restored system starts cleanly without additional fixes, reducing restore time.
LRS (locally redundant, 3 copies in one datacenter), GRS (geo-redundant, default, replicates to secondary region), and ZRS (zone-redundant, replicates across availability zones within same region).
For Azure Blobs, operational backup is continuous and stored locally in the source storage account; vaulted backup copies data to the Recovery Services vault for stronger isolation.

exam_focus_points: GRS is the default and recommended replication option for Recovery Services vaults
Recovery Services vault vs Backup vault — which workloads use which vault type
MARS agent backs up files/folders/system state; backup extensions back up entire Azure VMs
Azure Backup does not limit inbound/outbound data transfer except for offline initial backup via Azure Import/Export
Application-consistent backups ensure recovery points have all data needed without additional fixes
Azure Backup supports retention of PostgreSQL backups for up to 10 years
ZRS guarantees data residency and resiliency within the same region with no downtime
Backup storage is automatically managed with a pay-as-you-use model — no cost for on-premises storage devices
similar_concepts: Azure Storage redundancy (LRS/GRS/ZRS) at the storage account level
common_mistakes: Confusing Recovery Services vault with Backup vault — newer workloads like Managed Disks and AKS use Backup vault, not Recovery Services vault
Assuming LRS is the default replication — GRS is the default and recommended option
Confusing Azure Backup with Azure Site Recovery — Backup is for data protection/restore, Site Recovery is for disaster recovery and VM replication/failover
Thinking MARS agent backs up entire VMs — it only backs up files, folders, and system state; full VM backup uses backup extensions
Assuming Azure Backup charges for data transfer — inbound/outbound data transfer is unlimited and free except for offline import via Azure Import/Export
